paper_title: Adaptive Segment-level Reward: Bridging the Gap Between Action and Reward Space in Alignment

method.name: Adaptive Message-wise RLHF
key_features: Identifies key signals through rewards and generalized advantages during generation
Allows adaptive partitioning of samples into sub-sequences
Provides flexible control over gradient propagation

improvements.adversarial_samples: 10% improvement in success rate compared to sample-wise approach

error_sources: Coarse-grained nature of reward signals compared to actual rewards
Inherent stochastic errors within the reward model itself
optimization_strategies: Reduce granularity of coarse-grained reward signals
Use overall reward of longer sequences to represent individual tokens/subsequences
key_findings: Need to align information density gap within sequences with reward signals
Token-wise rewards exhibit significant fluctuations and high noise levels
Step-wise approach allows tokens at same step to have different rewards

title: Adaptive Segment-level Reward: Bridging the Gap Between Action and Reward Space in Alignment
